# UX Checklist: Search, Accessibility, Deep Links, Docs Validation

**Purpose**: Validate DOC-010 UX requirement completeness, clarity, consistency, and measurable reviewer evidence for search, support anchors, responsive install workflows, and minimal browser smoke scope before implementation.
**Created**: 2026-06-19
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Does the spec define the support-ready anchor inventory or scope rules for install, recovery, troubleshooting, generated reference, glossary, and release workflow answers? [Resolved, Spec §Support Anchor Inventory Scope, Plan §Phase 1 Design Summary]
- [x] CHK002 Does the spec define the route-to-critical-journey mapping that explains why the six-route Playwright smoke set is small but representative? [Resolved, Spec §Browser Smoke Route Rationale, Plan §Phase 1 Design Summary]
- [ ] CHK003 Are search discovery requirements defined for install, recovery, troubleshooting, reference, glossary, and release workflow guidance from existing navigation, search, or linked sections? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004, Spec §SC-002]
- [ ] CHK004 Are mobile and desktop behavior requirements defined for the install workflow route without requiring a broad visual snapshot suite? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-010, Spec §SC-003]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK005 Are stable deep-link conventions clear enough to distinguish intentional anchor changes from accidental heading drift? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-003, Data Model §Deep Link Anchor]
- [ ] CHK006 Is "no more than three meaningful interactions per topic" defined with enough topic and entry-point detail to be reviewable? [Clarity, Spec §SC-002]
- [ ] CHK007 Are Playwright baseURL responsibilities clear enough to keep smoke routes logical while still supporting the deployed `/racecraft-plugins-public` prefix? [Clarity, Spec §FR-010, Contract §Browser Smoke]
- [ ] CHK008 Are representative deep-link smoke expectations distinct from deterministic full link and anchor validation? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007, Spec §Assumptions, Contract §Docs Validation Path]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK009 Do search, glossary, reference, troubleshooting, and release workflow requirements consistently reuse existing routes rather than implying a new docs-quality route or search replacement? [Consistency, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-004, Research §Keep Starlight Search And Existing Routes]
- [ ] CHK010 Does the minimal smoke route list align across the spec, plan, browser-smoke contract, and quickstart? [Consistency, Spec §FR-010, Plan §Technical Context, Contract §Browser Smoke, Quickstart §Browser Smoke Review]
- [ ] CHK011 Do support-anchor requirements align with generated-reference and docs-quality validation ownership? [Consistency, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-007, Data Model §Deep Link Anchor, Contract §Docs Validation Path]
- [ ] CHK012 Do install workflow UX requirements align with accessibility evidence requirements for keyboard, status, fallback, and responsive behavior? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005, Spec §Accessibility Review Requirements, Quickstart §Manual Evidence]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK013 Can support findability success be objectively judged across install, recovery, troubleshooting, reference, glossary, and release workflow topics? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002]
- [ ] CHK014 Can browser smoke evidence be judged as representative without becoming a broad visual regression, exhaustive deep-link, or full accessibility certification suite? [Measurability, Spec §SC-006, Contract §Browser Smoke]
- [ ] CHK015 Can reviewer evidence connect changed docs surfaces to route coverage, search/deep-link assertions, manual UX/accessibility notes, known gaps, and rollback/fallback notes? [Measurability, Spec §FR-013, Spec §PR Review Packet Requirements]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK016 Are support responder scenarios covered for sharing stable install, troubleshooting, reference, glossary, and release workflow links? [Coverage, User Story 1, Spec §FR-002]
- [ ] CHK017 Are narrow viewport, desktop viewport, dynamic-fallback, and install workflow interaction scenarios addressed without expanding the route list? [Coverage, User Story 2, User Story 4, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK018 Are stale search results, renamed headings, removed generated reference anchors, and external platform claim drift addressed through validation or source-update guidance? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-012, Spec §SC-007]

## Notes

- Focus areas selected: existing search, glossary and support anchors, stable deep-link conventions, mobile and desktop install workflow behavior, Playwright route list and smoke assertions, and route-list scope discipline.
- Depth level: Standard reviewer gate.
- Actor/timing: PR reviewer before implementation tasks begin.

## Re-run Verification

- [x] CHK019 Is the support-ready anchor inventory now scoped by page family and support purpose without freezing every generated Starlight slug? [Resolved, Spec §Support Anchor Inventory Scope, Plan §Phase 1 Design Summary]
- [x] CHK020 Is the six-route Playwright smoke set now mapped to critical user journeys while preserving representative, non-exhaustive coverage? [Resolved, Spec §Browser Smoke Route Rationale, Plan §Phase 1 Design Summary]
